Remove unnecessary blank lines between prose and code blocks

When prose comments directly precede an example/test/it block, suppress
the blank line that was previously inserted between them. This improves
the visual presentation of the generated markdown.

The blank line is only suppressed when prose is immediately followed by
a code block. If there are describe() nodes in between, the blank line
is preserved to maintain proper markdown formatting.

Users can still explicitly add blank lines by creating separate prose
blocks with blank lines between comments.

commit.message:
fix: remove cancelOrphaned() that prevented timers from firing

cancelOrphaned() in TimerRegistry.start() marked ALL pending timers
as 'cancelled' in the DB before loadPending() ran, so the in-memory
pending map was always rebuilt empty. This broke:
- Timers created before start() (13 test failures)
- Timer persistence across registry restarts
- Repeat timer rescheduling

The fix removes cancelOrphaned() entirely. Stale timer cleanup is
already handled by cleanupOld() (7-day TTL), and fired timers for
non-existent agents are harmlessly ignored in the event handler.

Also changed loadPending() to merge with existing in-memory timers
rather than clearing first, so timers created before start() are
preserved.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add keyboard shortcuts and Space+drag panning with help overlay (#11)

This pull request adds comprehensive keyboard shortcut support to the
app, including a help dialog listing all shortcuts. It also refactors
zoom constants, updates the pan interaction to use Space+drag, and
improves file picker integration. The most important changes are grouped
below:

**Keyboard Shortcuts Integration:**

* Added the `useKeyboardShortcuts` hook to handle keyboard shortcuts for
zoom, pan, opening/closing images, and toggling help; integrated it into
`App.tsx`.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-37dcb95f5df3eade45825cacbd6c4d4fa7474f3f908b384e424abade70c8a851R1-R112)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R57-R62)
* Introduced the `KeyboardShortcutsHelp` component, which displays a
modal dialog listing all supported shortcuts, and wired it to the app
state.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-564de56907e8a52c9b50633d22969aad49fdbb820d5f98dad2d42d6708db1d6eR1-R58)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2R111-R113)
* Updated translations to include keyboard shortcut descriptions for the
help dialog.

**Zoom and Pan Improvements:**

* Moved zoom constants (`MIN_ZOOM`, `MAX_ZOOM`, `ZOOM_FACTOR`) to a
dedicated file and refactored their usage.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-f823bb654c4a0b6dd269672492c79ea103e87783b882aff59d026bd509b43a4aR1-R3)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dace83e490ae99e63c6df16550281f5908e84cce2f87512edb773336c3a6432eL3-R3)
* Changed pan interaction from middle mouse button to Space+left mouse
drag, improving usability and accessibility.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-dace83e490ae99e63c6df16550281f5908e84cce2f87512edb773336c3a6432eL36-R64)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-dace83e490ae99e63c6df16550281f5908e84cce2f87512edb773336c3a6432eL66-L85)

**File Picker Integration:**

* Modified `FilePickerButton` to accept an external input ref, allowing
keyboard shortcuts to trigger the file picker reliably.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-30ccb60f88a280fcd735fab02e8396672561de7f1581dac980831a3ea3a2dcc9L6-R16)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-26ad4b834941d9b19ebf9db8082bd202aaf72ea0ddea85f5a8a0cb3c729cc6f2L65-R75)
* Updated `App.tsx` to manage file picker and image closing via keyboard
shortcuts.

These changes together make the app more accessible and user-friendly,
enabling efficient navigation and actions through keyboard
shortcuts.Replace middle-mouse pan with Space+left drag (cursor changes
to grab/grabbing), add keyboard shortcuts (+/-/0/1 for zoom, Ctrl+O to
open, Escape to close, ? to toggle help overlay), and add a
KeyboardShortcutsHelp modal component with i18n strings.

* Replace step(n)/goto(n) with unified jump(n, relative:) function

- Replace `step(n)` and `goto(n)` with a single `jump(n, relative: false)`
- `relative: true` (relative mode): advances counter by n (n != 0, can be negative)
- `relative: false` (absolute mode, default): flush hidden content, set counter to n (n >= 1)
- `#pause` = `jump(1, relative: true)`, `#meanwhile` = `jump(1)` (sugar)
- Update all 3 parsers to handle `touying-jump` kind with relative field
- Update exports: remove step/goto, add jump
- Update tests: section renamed "Jump", use jump() syntax
- Update reference images for pages 32-44

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix negative relative jump not flushing hidden-parts at correct position

When jump(-1, relative: true) takes the subslide counter from a hidden
zone back into a visible zone, flush accumulated hidden-parts immediately
at that point (not deferred to the end) to preserve correct content order.

Fixes:
- _parse-touying-reducer: flush on re-entry to visible zone
- inner children loop in _parse-content-into-results-and-repetitions: same

Add test case for jump(-1, relative: true) in CeTZ canvas.

Co-authored-by: OrangeX4 <34951714+OrangeX4@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix max-repetitions not updated on positive relative jump before negative jump

When jump(+1, relative: true) raised repetitions to 2 and jump(-1, relative:
true) brought it back to 1, max-repetitions was never captured at the peak.
The final calc.max(1, 1)=1 caused only 1 subslide to be generated.

Fix: update max-repetitions immediately after every relative jump in both
_parse-touying-reducer and the inner children loop.

The "negative relative in CeTZ" test now correctly produces 2 subslides
(46 total pages, was 45 before this fix).

Cycle 20: E2E browser test infra — fix Kestrel startup, login flow, BFF checkout gaps, and SignalR antiforgery (#177)

* Initial plan

* Fix E2E test infrastructure, BFF gaps, checkout bugs, and order confirmation bug

- Add BFF proxy endpoints to Storefront.Api for checkout steps:
  ProvideCheckoutShippingAddress, SelectCheckoutShippingMethod, ProvideCheckoutPaymentMethod
- Add GetOrderView endpoint to Storefront.Api (fixes OrderConfirmation showing 'not found')
- Add GetOrderAsync to IOrdersClient interface and all stub implementations
- Fix OrdersClient.CompleteCheckoutAsync to parse orderId from response (not return checkoutId)
- Fix Checkout.razor: route all checkout steps through StorefrontApi BFF (remove Orders.Api bypass)
- Fix Checkout.razor: add MudStepper auto-advance with try/catch exception handling
- Fix Checkout.razor: add always-visible order summary sidebar with data-testid attributes
- Add full address fields to AddressSummary for shipping address resolution
- Update E2ETestFixture to use built-in UseKestrel() support (ASP.NET Core 10+)

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add diagnostic logging to Checkout.razor OnInitializedAsync; fix Login.razor ValidateAsync; fix LoginPage timing

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix MudSelect address option selection in CheckoutPage - use has-text locator after popup opens

The CI failure (7/8 tests timing out at 30s) was caused by
GetByRole(AriaRole.Option, { Name = "Home" }) not matching the
rendered MudSelectItem text "Home - 123 Main St, Seattle, WA 98101".

Fix: wait for the MudBlazor popup to open ([role='option'] visible),
then use [role='option']:has-text('Home') for partial text matching.
Also wait for AddressSelect to be visible before clicking to open it.

Co-authored-by: erikshafer <12145838+erikshafer@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: disable antiforgery on SignalR hub to fix E2E test failures

ASP.NET Core 10 enables antiforgery token validation on SignalR hubs
by default. WebSocket connections have built-in CORS protection that
already prevents CSRF attacks, making HTML form antiforgery redundant
for SignalR.

The E2E tests were seeing repeated 400 errors:
  'A valid antiforgery token was not provided with the request'

This caused SignalR negotiation to fail, breaking all real-time
order status update scenarios in the checkout flow E2E suite.

Fixes: Storefront.E2ETests SignalR connection establishment step

* docs: Add E2E Playwright testing skill document

Adds docs/skills/e2e-playwright-testing.md covering all patterns from
the Cycle 20 Storefront.E2ETests implementation:

- Real Kestrel servers via WebApplicationFactory (UseKestrel(0) ordering rule)
- Two-server architecture: Storefront.Web → Storefront.Api → stubs + TestContainers
- Page Object Model with data-testid selectors (MudBlazor-safe)
- MudSelect animated popover interaction pattern
- Login flow timing with Blazor forceLoad navigation race
- Stub coordination for deterministic IDs across cart→checkout boundary
- localStorage injection for pre-seeded browser state
- Wolverine InvokeMessageAndWaitAsync for SignalR E2E scenarios
- SignalR antiforgery fix for ASP.NET Core 10+
- Full Reqnroll hook lifecycle (BeforeTestRun / BeforeScenario / AfterScenario)
- Playwright tracing on failure
- WellKnownTestData and ScenarioContextKeys patterns
- Common pitfalls with concrete fixes
- Checklist for new E2E scenarios

Also updates docs/skills/README.md and CLAUDE.md to reference the new doc.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
